explanation: Krishnadevaraya, a prominent ruler of the Vijayanagara Empire, granted permission to the Portuguese to build a fort at Bhatkal in 1510. This was part of his policy to foster trade relations with foreign powers.

The Indian National Congress was indeed founded in 1885, and its first session was presided over by a British civilian, George Yule. This highlights the early involvement of foreigners in the Indian nationalist movement.

Chak (Chak) dance is recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage intangible cultural heritage. The other options, Durga Puja, Garba dance, and Kumbh Mela, are significant cultural festivals but are not currently listed as UNESCO World Heritage Sites in the same category.

Dr. Rajendra Prasad presided over the Constituent Assembly, and B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee. Sachidananda Sinha was the interim president of the Constituent Assembly for its first meeting, not its president throughout.

The Constituent Assembly was elected on the basis of indirect election by the Provincial Legislative Assemblies, as per the Cabinet Mission Plan of 1946. The Government of India Act, 1935, did not make provisions for the election of members to the Constituent Assembly.

Dr. Shyama Prasad Mukherjee founded the Bharatiya Jana Sangh (later part of the BJP), and Jagjivan Ram was a prominent leader of the Indian National Congress and founded the Jagjivan Ram Party. The question asks for parties founded by them respectively, and the options suggest a combination of parties associated with them.

The matching of ranks in the Indian Defence forces is as follows: Brigadier (Army) is equivalent to Air Commodore (Air Force) and Commodore (Navy). Major General (Army) is equivalent to Air Vice Marshal (Air Force) and Vice Admiral (Navy). Major (Army) is equivalent to Squadron Leader (Air Force) and Lieutenant Commander (Navy).
As of December 2023, three Delimitation Commissions have been constituted by the Government of India. These were established in 1952, 1963, and 2002, with the most recent one being active.
The Constitution (71st Amendment) Act, 1992, amended the Eighth Schedule to include Konkani, Manipuri, and Nepali. Maithili was included in a later amendment.

Inter-State quarantine is a subject that falls under the Union List, meaning it is under the jurisdiction of the Central government. Trade and commerce, and migration are subjects that have provisions under both Union and State lists, with specific regulations.

Part IX A of the Constitution deals with Municipalities, and Part XVIII contains the Emergency provisions. Provisions related to the amendment of the Constitution are given in Article 368, which is in Part XX of the Constitution.

The Ethics Committee in the Lok Sabha was initially an ad-hoc committee and later became a standing committee. Only a Member of the Lok Sabha can make a complaint regarding the unethical conduct of another Member. The committee can take up matters referred to it by the Speaker or the House.
